description: Perform a quantitative inclusion analysis on the metallographic cross-section image located at ~/Fiji_Data/raw/particles/specimen_micrograph.tif. Calibrate the image using the scale of 0.1725 µm/pixel (referenced in ~/Fiji_Data/raw/particles/calibration_info.txt). Segment the dark inclusion particles, excluding any noise smaller than 5 µm². Measure the Area, Perimeter, Circularity, Solidity, Feret's Diameter, and Aspect Ratio for all valid particles and save the detailed results to ~/Fiji_Data/results/particles/particle_measurements.csv. Generate a size distribution histogram of particle areas and save it to ~/Fiji_Data/results/particles/size_distribution.png. Produce an annotated image showing particle outlines and a scale bar (10-50 µm) at ~/Fiji_Data/results/particles/annotated_micrograph.png. Finally, create a summary report at ~/Fiji_Data/results/particles/inclusion_report.txt including the total particle count, mean area (µm²), area fraction (%), the number of particles exceeding 500 µm², and a QC result (PASS if 5 or fewer particles exceed 500 µm², otherwise FAIL).
